annotator_1.text: Chemical reaction networks without hidden layers can solve tasks for which spiking neural networks require hidden layers.
annotator_1.source_quote: We mathematically prove that chemical reaction networks without hidden layers can solve tasks for which spiking neural networks require hidden layers.

annotator_1.text: A certain reaction network without hidden layers can learn a classification task previously proved to be achievable by a spiking neural network with hidden layers.
annotator_1.source_quote: Specifically, we prove that a certain reaction network without hidden layers can learn a classification task previously proved to be achievable by a spiking neural network with hidden layers.

annotator_1.text: The proposed chemical reaction network solves the handwritten digit classification task more accurately than a spiking neural network with hidden layers.
annotator_1.source_quote: we show that it solves the task more accurately and efficiently than a spiking neural network with hidden layers

annotator_1.text: The proposed chemical reaction network solves the handwritten digit classification task more efficiently than a spiking neural network with hidden layers.
annotator_1.source_quote: we show that it solves the task more accurately and efficiently than a spiking neural network with hidden layers

annotator_2.text: Chemical reaction networks without hidden layers can solve tasks for which spiking neural networks require hidden layers.
annotator_2.source_quote: We mathematically prove that chemical reaction networks without hidden layers can solve tasks for which spiking neural networks require hidden layers.

annotator_2.text: The proof uses the deterministic mass-action kinetics formulation of chemical reaction networks.
annotator_2.source_quote: Our proof uses the deterministic mass-action kinetics formulation of chemical reaction networks.

annotator_2.text: A certain reaction network without hidden layers can learn a classification task previously proved to be achievable by a spiking neural network with hidden layers.
annotator_2.source_quote: Specifically, we prove that a certain reaction network without hidden layers can learn a classification task previously proved to be achievable by a spiking neural network with hidden layers.

annotator_2.text: The authors provide analytical regret bounds for the global behavior of the chemical reaction network.
annotator_2.source_quote: We provide analytical regret bounds for the global behavior of the network

annotator_2.text: The authors analyze the asymptotic behavior of the chemical reaction network.
annotator_2.source_quote: We provide analytical regret bounds for the global behavior of the network and analyze its asymptotic behavior

annotator_2.text: The authors analyze the Vapnik-Chervonenkis dimension of the chemical reaction network.
annotator_2.source_quote: We provide analytical regret bounds for the global behavior of the network and analyze its asymptotic behavior and Vapnik-Chervonenkis dimension.

annotator_2.text: The proposed chemical reaction network can classify handwritten digits in pixel images.
annotator_2.source_quote: In a numerical experiment, we confirm the learning capacity of the proposed chemical reaction network for classifying handwritten digits in pixel images

annotator_2.text: The chemical reaction network solves the handwritten digit classification task more accurately than a spiking neural network with hidden layers.
annotator_2.source_quote: we show that it solves the task more accurately and efficiently than a spiking neural network with hidden layers.

annotator_2.text: The chemical reaction network solves the handwritten digit classification task more efficiently than a spiking neural network with hidden layers.
annotator_2.source_quote: we show that it solves the task more accurately and efficiently than a spiking neural network with hidden layers.

annotator_2.text: The results provide motivation for machine learning in chemical computers.
annotator_2.source_quote: This provides a motivation for machine learning in chemical computers

annotator_2.text: The results provide a mathematical explanation for how biological cells might exhibit more efficient learning behavior within biochemical reaction networks than neuronal networks.
annotator_2.source_quote: This provides a motivation for machine learning in chemical computers and a mathematical explanation for how biological cells might exhibit more efficient learning behavior within biochemical reaction networks than neuronal networks.
